mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates the Federal Reserve is behind the curve, leading to expectations of aggressive future tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If, during identified post-shock recovery periods, a 5% annualized growth in INDPRO does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above a certain level indicates strong consumer and business confidence, stimulating economic activity and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 600 (thousands of units) for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances where HOUST sustains above 600k units for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month change in PAYEMS over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical level, it signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation or overheating, which increases borrowing costs and typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If, after FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of subsequent trading days for a sustained period of 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing GDP growth and persistent inflation indicates stagflationary pressures, where economic output stagnates while prices rise, eroding corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where GDP growth is below 1% annualized and CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3%.
